panel.strength: The plan correctly refuses to assert a cleared pathway, and accurately labels QXC/DEN220058 as adjacent (a behavioral therapeutic) rather than an on-point risk-prediction predicate.

critiques.issue: The entire specification is built on an INFERRED intended claim that the team has not confirmed; a prognostic-CDS claim versus an action-directing SaMD claim changes endpoints, comparator, and pathway.
critiques.evidence_basis: The plan itself flags the claim as 'INFERRED — team to confirm'; no on-point 510(k)/De Novo record for a 5-year T2DM risk classifier appears in the retrieved openFDA evidence, so device status cannot be inherited from a precedent.

critiques.issue: Device-status determination (non-device CDS under Cures Act vs. SaMD requiring De Novo) is left unresolved, yet it must be settled before the primary endpoint and study design are locked.
critiques.evidence_basis: Only adjacent device codes were retrieved (QXC/DEN220058 therapeutic; OIF autoantibody assays K171731/K073590/K220085; OYC insulin pump); the Compass Health MediPress recall Z-1343-2019 (marketing a change without proper 510(k)) shows the concrete cost of misjudging device status.

critiques.issue: No validated comparator risk score is named against which the model would be benchmarked, leaving 'added value over existing practice' undefined.
critiques.evidence_basis: No head-to-head comparator or established risk-score record appears in the retrieved literature or device set; the plan only gestures at 'an existing validated risk score' generically.

panel.strength: The plan appropriately makes calibration (slope/intercept, E/O, calibration curve) the dominant metric for a risk-communication tool and refuses to import single-cohort AUROC figures as targets.

critiques.issue: No sample size or incident-event count is computed; a prognostic validation is bound by number of events, and the plan defers the entire calculation to a later step.
critiques.evidence_basis: The plan states 'No sample size or event count was retrieved from any record'; indeed no retrieved study reports a transferable N or EPV justification, so the binding precision calculation remains unperformed.

critiques.issue: Subgroups are named but no minimum per-subgroup event counts or pre-specified precision targets are set, so subgroup calibration risks being underpowered and effectively post-hoc.
critiques.evidence_basis: Retrieved single-population studies (e.g., PPR:PPR1093984, Libya cohort) show performance is population-bound; no retrieved record supplies subgroup event thresholds, so these must be pre-computed rather than asserted.

critiques.issue: No external numeric performance target (discrimination or calibration) is anchored, leaving pass/fail criteria to be judgment calls at analysis time.
critiques.evidence_basis: The plan notes 'No FDA or society-endorsed numeric performance threshold... was retrieved'; retrieved modeling papers (PMID 34574055, PMID 31615566, W4388982729, W3038710713) report only internal discrimination without calibration or net benefit.

panel.strength: The plan honestly scopes out outcome-improvement certification (correctly stating that requires a prospective interventional design) and explicitly flags automation bias in clinicians over-trusting the score.

critiques.issue: The operating thresholds that would trigger a clinical action (preventive counseling vs. confirmatory testing) are described as 'to be pre-registered' but never defined, so it is unproven the risk output actually changes clinical management or offers actionable lead time.
critiques.evidence_basis: No retrieved study demonstrates that a diabetes-risk output alters clinical decisions; the retrieved literature is diagnosis/prediction modeling (PMID 34574055, PMID 31615566, PMID 28138367) with no deployment or action-linkage evidence, and no RCT for this indication was retrieved.

critiques.issue: Alert fatigue and workflow integration in busy primary-care practices are mentioned only as monitoring items, not designed-for constraints (e.g., expected flag rate at chosen thresholds).
critiques.evidence_basis: No retrieved record characterizes primary-care alert burden for a screening risk model; the concern rests on the absence of any workflow/fit study in the retrieved evidence.

critiques.issue: The HbA1c-discordance confounders (anemia, hemoglobinopathy, CKD) are named as label/physiology threats but no clinical workflow is specified for flagging affected patients at the point of use.
critiques.evidence_basis: The plan lists these confounders as capping label reliability; no retrieved study quantifies them for this model, so a point-of-care handling rule cannot be inherited and must be designed.

fix_before_submission: Confirm the intended clinical claim (risk-stratification CDS vs. action-directing SaMD) with the team before finalizing any endpoint or pathway.
Obtain regulatory-counsel determination of device status (non-device CDS vs. SaMD/De Novo) prior to locking study design.
Compute and pre-register the validation sample size driven by required incident-event count and calibration/C-statistic precision (e.g., pmsampsize).
Set minimum per-subgroup event counts and pre-specified precision targets for each named subgroup calibration analysis.
Define and pre-register the numeric operating thresholds that trigger clinical action (counseling vs. confirmatory testing) and demonstrate their actionability/lead-time.
